entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: The crop whose growth and grain yield were enhanced by the application of phosphate-solubilizing bacteria

entities.name: PHOSPHATE-SOLUBILIZING BACTERIA
entities.description: Bacteria capable of solubilizing phosphate, used to promote plant growth and increase biomass

entities.name: PLANT BIOMASS
entities.description: A growth trait measured as dry weight, which increased significantly with the application of specific bacterial strains

entities.name: PAPER TOWEL METHOD
entities.description: A screening method used to identify bacterial strains with phosphate-solubilizing ability

entities.name: SERRATIA MARCESCENS EB 67
entities.description: A specific bacterial strain that significantly increased plant biomass and grain yield in maize

entities.name: PSEUDOMONAS SP. CDB 35
entities.description: A specific bacterial strain that significantly increased plant biomass and grain yield in maize

entities.name: GLASSHOUSE CONDITIONS
entities.description: A controlled environment where the effects of bacterial strains on plant biomass were tested

entities.name: FIELD CONDITIONS
entities.description: A natural environment where the effects of bacterial strains on plant biomass and grain yield were tested

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: A productivity trait of maize that was increased through seed treatment with bacterial strains

entities.name: SEED TREATMENT
entities.description: The method of applying bacterial strains to maize seeds before sowing

entities.name: BUFFERED ROCK PHOSPHATE MEDIUM
entities.description: A specialized medium used to trace and recover bacterial populations from the maize rhizosphere

entities.name: RHIZOSPHERE
entities.description: The soil zone surrounding the maize roots from which bacterial populations were traced
